syllabus_desc: Describe the requirements to hold an aviation document, as laid down in the CA Act 2023, sections 55 and 78
concept: To hold any aviation document - whether it's a licence, certificate of airworthiness, or operational approval - you need to meet the legislative requirements set out in the Civil Aviation Act 2023. Section 55 establishes the fundamental requirement: you must have a current, valid document issued by the Director. Section 78 covers the application process and what the Director needs to be satisfied with before issuing. Think of it this way: just meeting the technical qualifications isn't enough - you've got to have the paperwork, it's got to be current, and the Director's had to formally approve and issued it. This is why checking your certificate's validity date before every flight matters.
exam_tip: You'll likely be asked to identify what makes a document legally valid. The key is formal issue by the Director under the CA Act - self-assessment or informal approval doesn't cut it.
key_facts: Aviation documents must be issued by the Director under CA Act 2023, section 55
You cannot legally operate without a current, valid document
Section 78 outlines the Director's powers in approving applications
The document must be in your possession when you are operating
A lophole by validity date is a common reason for training accidents
You cannot continue to operate after your certificate has expired
Your CoW is responsible for restoring your certificate to valid status
